text:
Wild Luck Alexandrite (, Ki Un Arekisandoraito) is a ring found in Greed Island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 153 

Appearance

The ring itself is never shown in the manga, but the illustration on the card shows a large jewel and ornamentation on each side of the ring. In the 1999 anime adaptation, the gemstone's color is teal; in the 2011 anime adaptation, its color is red.

Overview

Wild Luck Alexandrite is an item that can be obtained if a player gives everything the Sick Villagers ask for and then asks for nothing in return. A Paladin's Necklace is needed to cure their illnesses. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 135 

Plot

Greed Island arc

When Gon, Killua, and Biscuit initially encounter the Sick Villagers and learn their story, the two boys give them 80,000 and their overshirts, expecting an item in return. However, the villagers give them nothing. 

Genthru tells Sub and Bara that they can take "Wild Luck Alexandrite" from someone who has it. After obtaining a Paladin's Necklace, Gon wonders if the Sick Villagers were cursed and if the necklace will work on them. Biscuit points out that they aren't cards, but Killua thinks that it's worth a try. Gon and Killua ask the villagers if they will trust them and put their lives in their hands. Taken aback, the village leader says that they gave them everything and asked for nothing in return, replying that they will trust them. The villagers then turn into "Healthy Villagers" and celebrate being cured, presenting Gon with the "Wild Luck Alexandrite" card. 

During the meeting with Kazsule's alliance, Killua reveals that while they have "Wild Luck Alexandrite", it wouldn't be practical to monopolize it. The other members are surprised and wonder if someone else already has it, learning that five other players do. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 154 

After Gon introduces himself, Genthru says he saw that he has "Wild Luck Alexandrite". Discussing the situation with his team, Tsezguerra says that their "Voucher" will immediately transform into "Angel's Breath", with just "Wild Luck Alexandrite" left. Barry and Rodriot interject, saying that they will get the latter first because they will have bought Gon's team three weeks of time. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 17, Chapter 170 

Gon tells Genthru that he has 102 cards and 71 unique specified slot cards, and Genthru confirms that Gon is carrying both "Plot of Beach" and "Wild Luck Alexandrite". Hunter × Hunter - Volume 18, Chapter 176 

Card

The "Wild Luck Alexandrite" card's designated number is 75 and its rank-limit A-20. 

Trivia

Alexandrite is a variety of the mineral or gemstone chrysoberyl. This variety displays a color change from greenish to reddish (alexandrite effect) dependent upon the nature of ambient lighting.

Anime and Manga Differences

The item itself is exclusively shown in the 2011 anime adaptation; Hunter × Hunter - Episode 67 (2011) in the manga and 1999 anime adaptation it is seen only in its card illustration. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 82 (1999) 
While in the manga its color is not revealed, the gemstone's color differs in the anime adaptations:
in the 1999 series, it is teal; 
in the 2011 series, it is red.
